2603.16672.title: CritiSense: Critical Digital Literacy and Resilience Against Misinformation
2603.16672.paper_summary: Misinformation on social media undermines informed decision-making and public trust. Prebunking offers a proactive complement by helping users recognize manipulation tactics before they encounter them in the wild. We present CritiSense, a mobile media-literacy app that builds these skills through short, interactive challenges with instant feedback. It is the first multilingual (supporting nine languages) and modular platform, designed for rapid updates across topics and domains. We report a usability study with 93 users: 83.9% expressed overall satisfaction and 90.1% rated the app as easy to use. Qualitative feedback indicates that CritiSense helps improve digital literacy skills. Overall, it provides a multilingual prebunking platform and a testbed for measuring the impact of microlearning on misinformation resilience. Over 3+ months, we have reached 300+ active users. It is freely available to all users on the Apple App Store (https://apps.apple.com/us/app/critisense/id6749675792) and Google Play Store (https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.critisense&hl=en). Demo Video: https://shorturl.at/CDcdc
2603.16672.paper_summary_zh: 社交媒體上的錯誤資訊削弱了知情決策和公眾信任。預防性反駁通過幫助用戶在遭遇操控策略之前識別這些策略，提供了一種主動的補充。我們推出了CritiSense，一款通過短小的互動挑戰和即時反饋來培養這些技能的移動媒體素養應用程式。這是第一個多語言（支持九種語言）和模組化的平台，設計用於快速更新各種主題和領域。我們報告了一項針對93名用戶的可用性研究：83.9%的人表達了整體滿意度，90.1%的人評價該應用程式易於使用。定性反饋表明，CritiSense有助於提高數位素養技能。總的來說，它提供了一個多語言的預防性反駁平台和一個測試微學習對抗錯誤資訊韌性影響的試驗場。在超過3個月的時間裡，我們已經達到了300多名活躍用戶。它在Apple App Store（https://apps.apple.com/us/app/critisense/id6749675792）和Google Play Store（https://play.google.com/store/apps/details?id=com.critisense&hl=en）上免費提供給所有用戶。演示視頻：https://shorturl.at/CDcdc
2603.16672.author: Firoj Alam et.al.
2603.16672.authors: Firoj Alam, Fatema Ahmad, Ali Ezzat Shahroor, Mohamed Bayan Kmainasi, Elisa Sartori, Giovanni Da San Martino, Abul Hasnat, Raian Ali
